arguments.task: Visit Podio official pages and extract evidence for: API docs existence and base/API overview, authentication methods for the API, rate limit notes, pricing/access or free trial information, and any official developer/support pages relevant to building integrations. Also search the official Podio site and official developer docs for these topics. Return exact page URLs and concise quotes/snippets for each finding. Then separately visit GitHub and search for an official or community MCP server for Podio, returning repository URLs if found.

input.thought: Start browser-based extraction from official Podio and related first-party pages, plus GitHub MCP search.

raw_result.description: Workspace collaboration and CRM platform with a public developer API for app, item, task, and organization data.
raw_result.auth_notes: Podio developer docs publicly expose API documentation and an auth section, but the exact supported auth flows could not be fully verified from the gathered extracts alone; OAuth2 is strongly indicated and legacy app authentication may also exist, so auth details need human confirmation.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public developer documentation is available and Podio pricing is publicly self-serve with a free trial path on the pricing page.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer site indicates a broad REST API covering core Podio entities and concepts, with navigation for API reference plus developer forums, partner extensions, and support resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No MCP server could be verified from the evidence gathered before stopping; treat as unverified and review manually.

raw_result.blocker: Authentication specifics, rate-limit policy, and MCP availability were not fully verified from the captured evidence and need manual confirmation before confidently shipping a toolkit.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Rate-limit details were not verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer portal exists at developers.podio.com. Official pricing page is on Progress domain, suggesting Podio is operated within the Progress product portfolio. Developer portal also references forums, partner extensions, and help/support resources.
raw_result.human_review_reason: User requested conclusion without further research, but the gathered evidence did not conclusively verify exact auth flows, rate limits, pricing tier implications for API access, or whether an MCP server already exists.
